questions.question: On which day was the 2001 movie The Fast and the Furious originally released?
given_answers: The Fast and the Furious premiered in the United States on **Friday, June 22, 2001**.
The Fast and the Furious was originally released on **June 22, 2001**.
The 2001 film **_The Fast and the Furious_** was originally released in theaters on **June 22, 2001**.

questions.question: Who plays Dominic Toretto?
questions.completed_question: Which actor plays the role of Dominic Toretto in the film The Fast and the Furious?
given_answers: Dominic Toretto is portrayed by **Vin Diesel** (born Mark Vincent Vincent) in the *Fast & Furious* franchise. He has played the character in every film of the series (except for a brief cameo in *Fast & Furious 7* where his scenes were filled in with stand‑ins after Diesel’s injury).

Dominic “Dom” Toretto is portrayed by **Vin Diesel** (born Mark Sinclair) in the *Fast & Furious* film series. He has played the character in every main installment since the franchise’s debut in 2001.
Dominic Toretto is portrayed by **Vin Diesel**.

questions.question: What number of these movies were made?

given_answers:
**Main‑line “Fast & Furious” films (the saga that follows Dominic Toretto’s story)**  

| # | Title (U.S. release year) | Release date (U.S.) |

| 7 | *Furious 7* (2015) | April 3 2015 |
| 8 | *The Fate of the Furious* (2017) | April 14 2017 |
| 9 | *F9* (2021) | June 25 2021 |
|10 | *Fast X* (2023) | May 19 2023 |
|11 | *Fast X: Part 2* (2025) | May 24 2025 |

### So, **eleven** main‑line “Fast & Furious” movies have been released (the eleventh, *Fast X: Part 2*, arrived in 2025).  

If you also count the official spin‑off:

| # | Spin‑off title | Release year |
|---|----------------|--------------|
| 1 | *Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw* | August 2021 |

Including that spin‑off would bring the total number of theatrical releases in the franchise to **twelve**.  

*(All dates are U.S. theatrical releases.)*

So, counting all theatrical releases—including the spin‑off *Hobbs & Shaw* and the two‑part finale *Fast X*—the franchise has **12 movies** released to date.

As of today (April 2026), the **_Fast & Furious_** franchise has had **eleven** theatrical films released:

questions.question: Did he direct Stealth?
questions.completed_question: Did Rob Cohen direct the 2005 film Stealth?
given_answers: Yes. Rob Cohen was also the director of **“Stealth”** (the 2005 science‑fiction action film starring Jamie Foxx, Jessica Alba and Stellan Skarsgård). The movie was released in early 2005 and, like *The Fast and the Furious*, was a big‑budget action‑oriented project that Cohen helmed.

Yes. **Rob Cohen** directed the 2005 sci‑fi action film **“Stealth.”** The movie stars **Jessica Simpson, Jamie Foxx,** and **Josh Duhamel**, and follows a trio of fighter pilots who must stop a rogue, AI‑controlled stealth aircraft. It was released in the United States on January 28 2005.

Yes. Rob Cohen also directed the 2005 science‑fiction action film **_Stealth_** (starring Jessica Alba, Josh Duhamel, and Jamie Foxx). The movie was released in August 2005 and is part of Cohen’s repertoire of high‑octane, effects‑driven films.

questions.question: Where does Lionel Messi come from?
given_answers: Lionel Messi was born in **Rosario, Argentina** on June 24 1987. He grew up in the city of Rosario before moving to Spain as a teenager to join FC Barcelona’s famed youth academy, La Masia. His Argentine roots remain a big part of his identity, and he has represented Argentina’s national team throughout his career.

Lionel Messi was born in **Rosario, Santa Fe Province, Argentina** on June 24 1987. He grew up there before moving to Spain as a teenager to join FC Barcelona’s youth academy, La Masia. While he has spent much of his professional career in Europe (Spain and later France), his national team allegiance has always been with Argentina, and he has captained the Argentine national side to major trophies such as the Copa America (2021) and the FIFA World Cup (2022).
Lionel Messi was born in **Rosario, Argentina** (specifically, in the city’s Barrio Villa Domínico on June 24 1987). He grew up there before moving to Spain to join FC Barcelona’s youth academy.

questions.question: does he have a wife?
questions.completed_question: Is the soccer player Lionel Messi married?
given_answers:
Yes. Lionel Messi is married to **Antonella Roccuzzo**. 

- **How they met:** They grew up together in Rosario, Argentina, and have known each other since childhood.  
- **Marriage:** They were married on **June 30 2017** in a ceremony in their hometown of Rosario.  
- **Family:** The couple have three sons—Thiago (born 2012), Mateo (born 2015) and Ciro (born 2018).  

Antonella is often seen supporting Messi at matches and events, and she runs her own business ventures, including a fashion line and a skincare brand.

Yes. Lionel Messi is married to **Antonela Roccuzzo**.  

- **How they met:** They grew up together in Rosario and have known each other since childhood.  
- **Marriage:** They were married in a private ceremony in Rosario on **June 30 2017**.  
- **Family:** The couple has three children—Thiago (born 2012), Mateo (born 2015), and Ciro (born 2018).  

Antonela Roccuzzo is often seen supporting Messi at matches and is active on social media, where she shares glimpses of their family life.

Yes. Lionel Messi is married to **Antonella Roccuzzo**.  

- **How they met:** They grew up together in Rosario; Antonella is a cousin of Messi’s childhood friend Luís Suárez.  
- **Engagement & marriage:** They announced their engagement in June 2017 and were married on **30 June 2017** in a ceremony in Rosario, Argentina.  
- **Family:** The couple has three children—Thiago (born 2012), Mateo (born 2015) and Ciro (born 2018).  

Antonella Roccuzzo, born 26 February 1988 in Rosario, runs a fashion and lifestyle brand and often appears with Messi at public events and charitable functions.

questions.question: did he service time in prison?
questions.completed_question: Did the soccer player Lionel Messi serve time in jail?
given_answers: No. Lionel Messi has never served time in prison. Throughout his career he has faced legal matters—most notably a 2016 tax‑fraud case in Spain—but those issues were resolved through fines and settlements, not incarceration. He was convicted of tax evasion in 2016, ordered to pay a multimillion‑euro fine, and later received a suspended prison sentence, which means he did not actually go to jail.

No. Lionel Messi has never served a term in prison.

The closest he has come to a legal penalty was a **tax‑evasion case in Spain**:

| Year | Charge | Verdict | Sentence |
|------|--------|---------|----------|
| 2016 | Defrauding the Spanish tax authorities of about €4.1 million by using offshore companies to hide image‑rights earnings | Guilty (both Messi and his father, Jorge Messi) | 21 months in prison **suspended** (i.e., no actual jail time), plus a €2 million fine for each of them |

Because the prison term was suspended, Messi did **not** spend any time behind bars. Since then, he has complied with the court‑ordered payments and has not faced any further criminal convictions.

No. Lionel Messi has never served time in prison.

He was **convicted in 2016** by a Spanish court for **tax evasion** related to image‑rights earnings through his company Barça Players. The court sentenced him to **21 months in prison**, but the sentence was **suspended** (i.e., he did not have to go to jail) and he was instead fined €​2 million, along with his former agent and a former club accountant. The suspended term means the conviction stands on his record, but he never actually served any custodial time.
